name: Pierogi Mushroom Filling
recipeInstructions:
Tradition calls for using Polish Mushrooms, also known as Polish Gold, which are very expensive and hard to find. I use a combination of Shitake and button mushrooms instead.
1. Soak Shitake mushrooms in warm water for at least an hour.
2. Chop 1 pound button mushrooms roughly and sauté them in butter until they cook down.
3. Chop the onion and add it to the pan with the mushrooms, cooking until they are soft.
4. Add the chopped Shitake mushrooms (including the soaking water) and simmer for 5-6 minutes until the liquid starts to evaporate. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Let the mixture cool slightly before using it as a filling for pierogis.
6. If you make the mushroom filling ahead of time, it will absorb more flavor from the cooking liquid.
When preparing the fillings:
1. Squeeze out excess moisture from the mushrooms to prevent them from leaking out of the dough.
2. For an additional layer, prepare mashed potatoes by cooling them slightly, cutting sharp cheddar cheese into small pieces, and mixing in a little dill, salt, and pepper with the potatoes. This creates a wonderful stuffing option.
